elements.alt: ablr logo

content.text: We will cover the following topics with 10-minute breaks on the hour.
Usability testing with assistive technology (screen reader, magnification, or alternative input devices)

elements.alt: ablr. logo
Screenshot of Microsoft Forms interface showing New Form and New Quiz buttons along with templates.

content.text: Works for simple forms but still needs some remediation in Adobe Acrobat.
Can use Document Accessibility Toolbar (Vision Australia)

elements.alt: Screenshot of MS Word ribbon showing the Accessibility tab with options like Set alt-text and Check Accessibility. Below it is a sample text and form fields for Billing Contact.

content.text: Prepare Form tool – Form field auto detection is hit or miss
Press and hold Control key and drag

elements.alt: Screenshot of Adobe Acrobat showing the Prepare Form tool with a sample Voter Registration Form.

sections.title: Set tab order in Adobe Acrobat

content.text: Make sure the form fields are in the order that you want them to be
Drag up or down to correct
Notice that having relevant form names will help here

elements.alt: A screenshot of a software interface showing a side panel with alignment and distribution tools, and a list of fields organized by page number.

content.text: Enter Tooltip (applies to both radio buttons)
Enter Choice for each radio button

elements.alt: Screenshot of Adobe Acrobat showing Radio Button Properties dialog box with General, Appearance, Position, Options, and Actions tabs. The General tab is open showing Name and Tooltip fields.

content.text: Use Preview button to test out form
To clear out your entries when you go back to Edit, Select the More Dropdown

elements.alt: Screenshot of Adobe Acrobat showing a form in Edit mode with the Preview button highlighted in the top right corner. The 'More' dropdown is also visible on the right sidebar.

content.text: Nest them appropriately within the tag structure – usually same paragraph tag as the text that corresponds with the field
Go to Accessibility tool – run scan
Notice that manually created form fields will not be tagged and will have errors in Tagged Annotations and Forms
Two ways to tag – Reading order panel, select Form Field (manual can be slow) OR Find > Select Unmarked Annotations > Find (will highlight element) > Tag Element (faster, but have to create Form tag)

elements.alt: Screenshot of Adobe Acrobat Accessibility Checker showing document issues and State Voter Registration Application form.
Ablr logo

sections.title: Step 6: Use a screen reader to check

content.text: This is an advanced step but provides the end user experience.
Can provide additional screen reader training.

elements.alt: Screenshot of NVDA Speech Viewer active on a State Voter Registration Application form.
Screenshot of right-clicking a desktop shortcut to open Properties.
Screenshot of NVDA Properties window showing the Shortcut key set to Ctrl + Alt + N.
Keyboard layout showing Caps Lock and N keys highlighted.
NVDA menu showing Exit option highlighted.
NVDA Welcome dialog showing 'Use CapsLock as an NVDA modifier key' checked.

content.text: Activate the CapsLock key to be your NVDA Modifier Key
Referred to as NVDA Key in Commands list

elements.alt: Keyboard highlighting Ctrl, Alt, and N keys.
NVDA application icon.

content.text: Turn on with Ctrl + Alt + N or double clicking the NVDA icon

sections.title: Customizing Settings – Voice and Speech Rate

elements.alt: NVDA menu opening Settings from Preferences.
NVDA Speech Settings dialogue box.
NVDA menu navigating to Settings.
NVDA Mouse Settings dialogue showing 'Enable mouse tracking' option.
NVDA menu showing Tools and Speech Viewer selected.
NVDA Speech Viewer window active.

content.text: Navigate Active Elements = Tab or Shift + Tab
Back to Top of Page = CTRL + Home
Select currently focused element = Enter (or Space)
Screen Reader Basics – NVDA (YouTube) is an older video, but parts are still valuable.

elements.alt: Mac System Preferences showing the Accessibility icon highlighted.
Accessibility preferences showing VoiceOver selected in the sidebar and the Enable VoiceOver option.
Mac keyboard with the Fn, Command, and F5 keys highlighted, as well as the Touch ID button in the top right.
Mac external keyboard highlighting the Command key and F5 key.

content.text: VO + Shift + down/up arrow = interact with content
VO + Command + H = Navigate by heading

elements.alt: Mac keyboard with the Control and Option keys highlighted.
Multiple screenshots of the VoiceOver Rotor menu on Mac showing lists for Web Spots, Tables, Landmarks, Links, and Headings.
iOS device settings showing the navigation path: Settings > Accessibility > VoiceOver. The final screen shows basic VoiceOver commands.

content.text: Use VoiceOver gestures to perform actions such as navigating and interacting with items on the screen.
Touch screen or drag finger – read screen
Double tap – select currently focused item
Two finger scrub back and forth - escape
Three finger triple tap – screen curtain

elements.alt: Screenshot of an iPhone displaying the Ablr website with the VoiceOver rotor interface active and set to Headings.
VoiceOver rotor interface dial with Links selected.
VoiceOver rotor interface dial with Form Controls selected.
VoiceOver rotor interface dial with Characters selected.
VoiceOver rotor interface dial with Words selected.
QR Code linking to Ablr resources or contact information.
